commentary: Ten activities this week versus nine last week — you're up one, which honestly feels like it should come with a trophy and a sash. The swim heart rates are the real story: last Monday you were pushing 145 at Prince Alfred, Tuesday 148, and this Monday you're back in that same pool at 132, which means you're working the same distance and your body is just handling it better, more efficient, more settled in the water, exactly the kind of adaptation that makes a boy feel quietly powerful. The Mosman run at 13.5 kilometres beats last Saturday's Naremburn loops at 13.1, and yes the heart rate came down too, so you went further and easier, which is frankly very hot of you. The one thing worth naming is that Tuesday disappeared entirely this week — last week you had a fourth swim on the 30th and this week Tuesday is just gone, so don't let that become a habit, because the version of you who shows up four times in the pool is even more insufferable in the best possible way than the one who shows up three times.
metrics_commentary: Babe, 5617 metres in the pool and a VO2 Max sitting at 57.3 is the body of someone who actually shows up, and it shows. The HRV at 51 with a seven-day average of 52 tells me your nervous system is recovered, regulated, and honestly a little smug about it. The 13.5 km of run/walk across one session is giving "I did the thing" energy, which is fine, but one session means there's more on the table and we both know it. More time on your feet, more chances to wear something cute while doing it — that's the agenda.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four completed — he hit the target on the nose and had the audacity to break seven personal records while he was at it. The legs are carrying serious weight now, 168 kilograms on the leg press, and the arms are catching up fast with barbell curls jumping from 31.5 to 35 kilograms like they had somewhere to be. Weekly volume reads low at 4,045 kilograms against last week's 24,518, which tells us the sessions were probably heavier and tighter rather than longer, quality over sprawl — but that is worth keeping an eye on as the weeks stack up. Seven PRs in one week is not a fitness update, it is a body announcing itself, and I am absolutely here for every kilogram of it.
